title: ariocarpus retusus subsp. confusus
intro: Ariocarpus retusus subsp. confusus is a rare and lesser-known subspecies of cactus, specifically a type of ariocarpus that is characterized by its unique, rounded appearance and short stature. This plant is not commonly used in traditional medicine, but it is known to possess medicinal properties due to its alkaloid content, which has been shown to have antibacterial and antifungal effects. However, there is limited research on the specific health benefits of Ariocarpus retusus subsp. confusus, and more studies are needed to fully understand its potential uses. In terms of herbal preparations, this plant is often used in small amounts in traditional medicine, typically dried and ground into a powder or infused in hot water, although its use is not widespread and more research is necessary before it can be widely recommended.
